zh.Studley, Warwickshire, England: 英國瓦維其可爾斯圖德利
zh.Stanford Rivers, Essex, England: 英國艾可斯坦福 瑞弗斯
zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabel Bourne於大約1336年出生在英國艾可斯坦福 瑞弗斯時，她的父親Sir Thomas of Ripon De Bourne58歲，母親Isabel de Gras23歲。她於1354年10月與Richard V Tempest Knt.在英國艾可斯坦福 瑞弗斯結婚。他們至少是4個兒子的父母。她去世於1421年8月13日在英國達勒姆享年86歲。

zh-hans.Studley, Warwickshire, England: 英国瓦維其可爾斯圖德利
zh-hans.Stanford Rivers, Essex, England: 英国艾可斯坦福 瑞弗斯
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Isabel Bourne于大约1336年出生在英国艾可斯坦福 瑞弗斯时，她的父亲Sir Thomas of Ripon De Bourne58岁，母亲Isabel de Gras23岁。她于1354年10月与Richard V Tempest Knt.在英国艾可斯坦福 瑞弗斯结婚。他们至少是4个儿子的父母。她去世于1421年8月13日在英国達勒姆享年86岁。

nameMeanings.name: Bourne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived beside a stream, from southern Middle English bourne, Old English burna, burne ‘spring, stream’, or a habitational name from a place called with this word, for example Bourn in Cambridgeshire or Bourne in Lincolnshire. In surnames the reference is often to an old stream called burna, surviving as the name of a farm. This word was replaced as the general word for a stream in southern dialects by Old English brōc (see Brook ) and came to be restricted in meaning to a stream flowing only intermittently, especially in winter.

variants.name: Burn
meanings: English (Northumberland and Durham): variant of   Bourne  .

variants.name: Born
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt, Otto, Heinz, Helmut, Berthold, Frieda, Friedrich, Fritz, Gerhard, Horst, Jutta.
English (of Norman origin): nickname from Old French borgne ‘one-eyed, squint-eyed’.

variants.name: Cockburn
meanings: Scottish (Midlothian) and northern English: habitational name from a place in Berwickshire named Cockburn, from Old English cocc ‘cock, rooster’ (or the related byname Cocca) + burna ‘stream’ (see Bourne ). This surname is traditionally pronounced Coburn.

variants.name: Bournes
meanings: English: variant of Bourne with plural or post-medieval exrescent -s.

variants.name: Brook
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived by a brook, stream, water-meadows or low, marshy ground, from Middle English brook, Old English brōc ‘brook, stream’; or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, such as Brook (Kent, Rutland) or Brooke (Norfolk).
North German: topographic name for someone who lived by a water meadow or marsh, from Low German brook (cognate with German Bruch ‘swamp’ and Old English brōc; see 1 above).
Americanized form of Dutch Broek: topographic name from broek, a cognate of 2 above.

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Isabel Bourne was born about 1336, in Stanford Rivers, Essex, England, her father, Sir Thomas of Ripon De Bourne, was 58 and her mother, Isabel de Gras, was 23. She married Richard V Tempest Knt. in October 1354, in Stanford Rivers, Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. She died on 13 August 1421, in Durham, England, at the age of 86.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isabel Bourne est née environ 1336, à Stanford Rivers, Essex, Angleterre, son père, Sir Thomas of Ripon De Bourne, avait 58 ans et sa mère, Isabel de Gras, avait 23 ans. Elle a épousé Richard V Tempest Knt. en octobre 1354, à Stanford Rivers, Essex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils. Elle est décédée le 13 août 1421, à Durham, Angleterre, à l’âge de 86 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Isabel Bourne um 1336 in Stanford Rivers, Essex, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Thomas of Ripon De Bourne 58 und ihre Mutter Isabel de Gras 23. Sie hat im Oktober 1354 in Stanford Rivers, Essex, England Richard V Tempest Knt. geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne. Sie starb am 13. August 1421 im Alter von 86 Jahren in Durham, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel Bourne è nata il circa 1336 nel Stanford Rivers, Essex, England, sua padre, Sir Thomas of Ripon De Bourne, aveva 58 anni e sua madre, Isabel de Gras, aveva 23 anni. Lei sposato Richard V Tempest Knt. nel ottobre 1354, nel Stanford Rivers, Essex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli. Morì il 13 agosto 1421, nel Durham, England, all'età di 86 anni.

ja.Stanford Rivers, Essex, England: イギリスエセックススタンフォード リバーズ
ja.lifeSummary: Isabel Bourneが約1336年にイギリスエセックススタンフォード リバーズで生まれた時，父Sir Thomas of Ripon De Bourneは58歳，母Isabel de Grasは23歳であった。1354年10月にイギリスエセックススタンフォード リバーズでRichard V Tempest Knt.と結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子の親であった。彼女は1421年8月13日にイギリスダルハムで86歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Isabel Bourne(이)가 대략 1336년 영국 에세스 스탠퍼드 라이버스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Thomas of Ripon De Bourne(은)는 58세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabel de Gras(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 1354년 10월 영국 에세스 스탠퍼드 라이버스에서 Richard V Tempest Knt.(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1421년 8월 13일 영국 뒤람에서 86세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel Bourne nasceu aproximadamente 1336, em Stanford Rivers, Essex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Thomas of Ripon De Bourne, tinha 58 anos e sua mãe, Isabel de Gras, tinha 23 anos. Ela casou-se com Richard V Tempest Knt. em outubro de 1354, em Stanford Rivers, Essex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos. Ela faleceu em 13 de agosto de 1421, em Durham, Inglaterra, com 86 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isabel Bourne родилась около 1336 г. в местности станфорд ривирс, эссекс, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Thomas of Ripon De Bourne было 58 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabel de Gras, 23. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard V Tempest Knt. октябрь 1354 г. в местности станфорд ривирс, эссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына. Она умерла 13 августа 1421 г. в местности дурам, Англия в возрасте 86 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Isabel Bourne nació aproximadamente 1336, en Stanford Rivers, Essex, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Thomas of Ripon De Bourne, tenía 58 años y su mamá, Isabel de Gras, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Richard V Tempest Knt. en octubre de 1354, en Stanford Rivers, Essex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos. Murió el 13 de agosto de 1421, en Durham, Inglaterra, a la edad de 86 años.
